credit-card.notes: Pennsylvania applies a 4-year limitation to written contracts including credit card debt. Pennsylvania's Fair Credit Extension Uniformity Act (FCEUA) extends FDCPA-like protections to original creditors.

medical.notes: Medical debts in Pennsylvania are subject to the 4-year limitation for written contracts.

auto-loan.notes: Auto loan deficiency balances in Pennsylvania follow the 4-year written contract limitation.

personal-loan.notes: Personal loans in Pennsylvania are subject to the 4-year limitation for written contracts.

student-loan.notes: Private student loans in Pennsylvania follow the 4-year limitation. Federal loans are exempt from state SOL for government collection.

mortgage-deficiency.notes: Mortgage deficiency claims in Pennsylvania are subject to the 4-year limitation. Pennsylvania courts have some restrictions on deficiency claims after non-judicial sales.

utility-bill.notes: Utility bill debts in Pennsylvania are subject to the 4-year limitation for written contracts.

regulatoryBody: Pennsylvania Department of Banking and Securities & Pennsylvania Attorney General
complaints: File a complaint with PA DOBS at dobs.pa.gov or the PA AG at attorneygeneral.gov. Pennsylvania's FCEUA and the Unfair Trade Practices and Consumer Protection Law provide additional consumer rights.
